heading: 9.7.14.6.2.2.Matrix Fragments for sparsemma.m16n8k32with.f16and.bf16types
content:
A warp executing sparse `mma.m16n8k32` with `.f16` / `.bf16` floating point type will compute
an MMA operation of shape `.m16n8k32`.

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warp so each thread of the warp holds
a fragment of the matrix.

* Multiplicand A:

  | .atype | Fragment | Elements |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.f16` / `.bf16` | A vector expression containing four `.b32` registers, with each register containing two non-zero `.f16` / `.bf16` elements out of 4 consecutive elements from matrix A. | Mapping of the non-zero elements is as described in [Sparse matrix storage](#warp-level-sparse-matrix-storage). |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 120](#sparse-mma-16832-f16-bf16-a).

  



[Figure: sparse-mma-16832-f16-bf16-A]
SPARSE MMA .m16n8k32 FRAGMENT LAYOUT FOR MATRIX A WITH .f16/.bf16 TYPE

=============================================================
1. MATRIX IDENTIFICATION
=============================================================
Matrix: A (input matrix)
Dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=32
Data type: .f16 or .bf16
Packing: Two f16/bf16 values packed per 32-bit register element

=============================================================
2. GRID STRUCTURE
=============================================================
Rows (matrix rows): 0, 1, 2, ..., 7, 8, 9, 10, ..., 15
Columns (K dimension, column groups): 0..3, 4..7, 8..11, 12..15, 16..19, 20..23, 24..27, 28..31
Threads: T0 through T31 (32 threads total)

=============================================================
3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING TABLE
=============================================================

Row\Column  | 0..3          | 4..7          | 8..11         | 12..15        | 16..19        | 20..23        | 24..27        | 28..31
------------|---------------|---------------|---------------|---------------|---------------|---------------|---------------|---------------
Row 0       | T0:{a0,a1}    | T1:{a0,a1}    | T2:{a0,a1}    | T3:{a0,a1}    | T0:{a4,a5}    | T1:{a4,a5}    | T2:{a4,a5}    | T3:{a4,a5}
Row 1       | T4:{a0,a1}    | T5:{a0,a1}    | T6:{a0,a1}    | T7:{a0,a1}    | T4:{a4,a5}    | T5:{a4,a5}    | T6:{a4,a5}    | T7:{a4,a5}
Row 2       | (continues pattern, threads T8-T11 with {a0,a1} / T8-T11 with {a4,a5})
...         | ...           | ...           | ...           | ...           | ...           | ...           | ...           | ...
Row 7       | T28:{a0,a1}   | T29:{a0,a1}   | T30:{a0,a1}   | T31:{a0,a1}   | T28:{a4,a5}   | T29:{a4,a5}   | T30:{a4,a5}   | T31:{a4,a5}
Row 8       | T0:{a2,a3}    | T1:{a2,a3}    | T2:{a2,a3}    | T3:{a2,a3}    | T0:{a6,a7}    | T1:{a6,a7}    | T2:{a6,a7}    | T3:{a6,a7}
Row 9       | T4:{a2,a3}    | T5:{a2,a3}    | T6:{a2,a3}    | T7:{a2,a3}    | T4:{a6,a7}    | T5:{a6,a7}    | T6:{a6,a7}    | T7:{a6,a7}
Row 10      | (continues pattern, threads T8-T11 with {a2,a3} / T8-T11 with {a6,a7})
...         | ...           | ...           | ...           | ...           | ...           | ...           | ...           | ...
Row 15      | T28:{a2,a3}   | T29:{a2,a3}   | T30:{a2,a3}   | T31:{a2,a3}   | T28:{a6,a7}   | T29:{a6,a7}   | T30:{a6,a7}   | T31:{a6,a7}

=============================================================
4. GROUPING PATTERNS
=============================================================
THREAD GROUPING (M dimension / rows):
- Rows 0-7:   Threads T0-T31 map in groups of 4 per row
  - Row 0: T0, T1, T2, T3
  - Row 1: T4, T5, T6, T7
  - Row 2: T8, T9, T10, T11 (implied by pattern)
  - Row 3: T12, T13, T14, T15 (implied)
  - Row 4: T16, T17, T18, T19 (implied)
  - Row 5: T20, T21, T22, T23 (implied)
  - Row 6: T24, T25, T26, T27 (implied)
  - Row 7: T28, T29, T30, T31
- Rows 8-15: Same thread assignment as rows 0-7 respectively, but different register elements

REGISTER ELEMENT GROUPING (K dimension / columns):
- Columns 0..15  (K positions 0-15):  register elements a0, a1 (for rows 0-7) or a2, a3 (for rows 8-15)
- Columns 16..31 (K positions 16-31): register elements a4, a5 (for rows 0-7) or a6, a7 (for rows 8-15)

=============================================================
5. REGISTER ELEMENT SUMMARY PER THREAD
=============================================================
Each thread holds 8 register elements total: a0, a1, a2, a3, a4, a5, a6, a7
Packed as pairs in 32-bit registers:
  - Register containing {a0, a1}: covers K columns 0..15, matrix rows 0..7
  - Register containing {a2, a3}: covers K columns 0..15, matrix rows 8..15
  - Register containing {a4, a5}: covers K columns 16..31, matrix rows 0..7
  - Register containing {a6, a7}: covers K columns 16..31, matrix rows 8..15

Each 32-bit register holds 2 f16 or bf16 values packed together.
Total registers per thread for matrix A: 4 registers (each holding 2 elements = 8 elements total).

=============================================================
6. COLUMN COVERAGE PER THREAD
=============================================================
Each thread covers 4 consecutive column positions within each column group (groups of 4 columns span 4 K-positions), and each thread appears in all 4 column positions within its group (columns 0..3, 4..7, 8..11, 12..15 all map to the same thread with the same register elements, indicating the 4 columns within a group correspond to the 4 threads in a row sharing the same register values — i.e., T0 appears at column 0, T1 at column 1, T2 at column 2, T3 at column 3 within the 0..3 group).

SPARSE MMA .m16n8k32 FRAGMENT LAYOUT FOR MATRIX A WITH .f16/.bf16 TYPE

=============================================================
1. MATRIX IDENTIFICATION
=============================================================
Matrix: A (input matrix)
Dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=32
Data type: .f16 or .bf16
Packing: Two f16/bf16 values packed per 32-bit register element

=============================================================
2. GRID STRUCTURE
=============================================================
Rows (matrix rows): 0, 1, 2, ..., 7, 8, 9, 10, ..., 15
Columns (K dimension, column groups): 0..3, 4..7, 8..11, 12..15, 16..19, 20..23, 24..27, 28..31
Threads: T0 through T31 (32 threads total)

=============================================================
3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING TABLE
=============================================================

Row\Column  | 0..3          | 4..7          | 8..11         | 12..15        | 16..19        | 20..23        | 24..27        | 28..31
------------|---------------|---------------|---------------|---------------|---------------|---------------|---------------|---------------
Row 0       | T0:{a0,a1}    | T1:{a0,a1}    | T2:{a0,a1}    | T3:{a0,a1}    | T0:{a4,a5}    | T1:{a4,a5}    | T2:{a4,a5}    | T3:{a4,a5}
Row 1       | T4:{a0,a1}    | T5:{a0,a1}    | T6:{a0,a1}    | T7:{a0,a1}    | T4:{a4,a5}    | T5:{a4,a5}    | T6:{a4,a5}    | T7:{a4,a5}
Row 2       | (continues pattern, threads T8-T11 with {a0,a1} / T8-T11 with {a4,a5})
...         | ...           | ...           | ...           | ...           | ...           | ...           | ...           | ...
Row 7       | T28:{a0,a1}   | T29:{a0,a1}   | T30:{a0,a1}   | T31:{a0,a1}   | T28:{a4,a5}   | T29:{a4,a5}   | T30:{a4,a5}   | T31:{a4,a5}
Row 8       | T0:{a2,a3}    | T1:{a2,a3}    | T2:{a2,a3}    | T3:{a2,a3}    | T0:{a6,a7}    | T1:{a6,a7}    | T2:{a6,a7}    | T3:{a6,a7}
Row 9       | T4:{a2,a3}    | T5:{a2,a3}    | T6:{a2,a3}    | T7:{a2,a3}    | T4:{a6,a7}    | T5:{a6,a7}    | T6:{a6,a7}    | T7:{a6,a7}
Row 10      | (continues pattern, threads T8-T11 with {a2,a3} / T8-T11 with {a6,a7})
...         | ...           | ...           | ...           | ...           | ...           | ...           | ...           | ...
Row 15      | T28:{a2,a3}   | T29:{a2,a3}   | T30:{a2,a3}   | T31:{a2,a3}   | T28:{a6,a7}   | T29:{a6,a7}   | T30:{a6,a7}   | T31:{a6,a7}

=============================================================
4. GROUPING PATTERNS
=============================================================
THREAD GROUPING (M dimension / rows):
- Rows 0-7:   Threads T0-T31 map in groups of 4 per row
  - Row 0: T0, T1, T2, T3
  - Row 1: T4, T5, T6, T7
  - Row 2: T8, T9, T10, T11 (implied by pattern)
  - Row 3: T12, T13, T14, T15 (implied)
  - Row 4: T16, T17, T18, T19 (implied)
  - Row 5: T20, T21, T22, T23 (implied)
  - Row 6: T24, T25, T26, T27 (implied)
  - Row 7: T28, T29, T30, T31
- Rows 8-15: Same thread assignment as rows 0-7 respectively, but different register elements

REGISTER ELEMENT GROUPING (K dimension / columns):
- Columns 0..15  (K positions 0-15):  register elements a0, a1 (for rows 0-7) or a2, a3 (for rows 8-15)
- Columns 16..31 (K positions 16-31): register elements a4, a5 (for rows 0-7) or a6, a7 (for rows 8-15)

=============================================================
5. REGISTER ELEMENT SUMMARY PER THREAD
=============================================================
Each thread holds 8 register elements total: a0, a1, a2, a3, a4, a5, a6, a7
Packed as pairs in 32-bit registers:
  - Register containing {a0, a1}: covers K columns 0..15, matrix rows 0..7
  - Register containing {a2, a3}: covers K columns 0..15, matrix rows 8..15
  - Register containing {a4, a5}: covers K columns 16..31, matrix rows 0..7
  - Register containing {a6, a7}: covers K columns 16..31, matrix rows 8..15

Each 32-bit register holds 2 f16 or bf16 values packed together.
Total registers per thread for matrix A: 4 registers (each holding 2 elements = 8 elements total).

=============================================================
6. COLUMN COVERAGE PER THREAD
=============================================================
Each thread covers 4 consecutive column positions within each column group (groups of 4 columns span 4 K-positions), and each thread appears in all 4 column positions within its group (columns 0..3, 4..7, 8..11, 12..15 all map to the same thread with the same register elements, indicating the 4 columns within a group correspond to the 4 threads in a row sharing the same register values — i.e., T0 appears at column 0, T1 at column 1, T2 at column 2, T3 at column 3 within the 0..3 group).


  Figure 120 Sparse MMA .m16n8k32 fragment layout for matrix A with `.f16`/`.bf16` type.

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =      groupID            for ai where  0 <= i < 2 || 4 <= i < 6
             groupID + 8        Otherwise

  col = [firstcol ... lastcol]  // As per the mapping of non-zero elements
                                // as described in Sparse matrix storage

  Where
  firstcol = threadID_in_group * 4          For ai where i <  4
             (threadID_in_group * 4) + 16   for ai where i >= 4
  lastcol  = firstcol + 3
  ```
* Multiplicand B:

  | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.f16` / `.bf16` | A vector expression containing four `.b32` registers, each containing two `.f16` / `.bf16` elements from matrix B. | b0, b1, b2, b3 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 121](#sparse-mma-16832-f16bf16-b).

  

[Figure: sparse-mma-16832-f16bf16-B]
SPARSE MMA .m16n8k32 FRAGMENT LAYOUT FOR MATRIX B WITH .f16/.bf16 TYPE

=============================================================
OVERVIEW
=============================================================
Matrix: B (input matrix)
Dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=32
Data type: .f16 or .bf16
Addressing: %laneid:{fragments}
This is the SPARSE variant of the m16n8k32 operation.

=============================================================
GRID STRUCTURE
=============================================================
Rows (labeled "Row\Column") represent matrix rows (0-31, but only specific rows shown)
Columns (0, 1, 2, ..., 7) represent the 8 columns of matrix B (N=8 dimension)
Threads T0-T31 are distributed across the grid cells

The table has 4 horizontal bands, each covering 8 rows of the matrix:
  Band 1: rows 0-7   (registers b0, b1)
  Band 2: rows 8-15  (registers b2, b3)
  Band 3: rows 16-23 (registers b4, b5)
  Band 4: rows 24-31 (registers b6, b7)

=============================================================
REGISTER PACKING
=============================================================
Each register entry shown as a 2-element column vector: {b_top, b_bottom}
Two f16 (or bf16) values are packed into a single 32-bit register.
Each thread holds 8 registers total (b0 through b7), organized as 4 pairs.

=============================================================
DETAILED THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING
=============================================================

BAND 1 (Matrix rows 0-7, register pair b0/b1):

Column 0:
  Rows 0-1:   T0  holds {b0, b1}
  Rows 6-7:   T3  holds {b0, b1}

Column 1:
  Rows 0-1:   T4  holds {b0, b1}
  Rows 6-7:   T7  holds {b0, b1}

Column 2:
  (implied continuation of pattern, threads T8/T11)

Columns 3-6: ... (ellipsis shown, pattern continues)

Column 7:
  Rows 0-1:   T28 holds {b0, b1}
  Rows 6-7:   T31 holds {b0, b1}

BAND 2 (Matrix rows 8-15, register pair b2/b3):

Column 0:
  Rows 8-9:   T0  holds {b2, b3}
  Rows 14-15: T3  holds {b2, b3}

Column 1:
  Rows 8-9:   T4  holds {b2, b3}
  Rows 14-15: T7  holds {b2, b3}

Column 7:
  Rows 8-9:   T28 holds {b2, b3}
  Rows 14-15: T31 holds {b2, b3}

BAND 3 (Matrix rows 16-23, register pair b4/b5):

Column 0:
  Rows 16-17: T0  holds {b4, b5}
  Rows 22-23: T3  holds {b4, b5}

Column 1:
  Rows 16-17: T4  holds {b4, b5}
  Rows 22-23: T7  holds {b4, b5}

Column 7:
  Rows 16-17: T28 holds {b4, b5}
  Rows 22-23: T31 holds {b4, b5}

BAND 4 (Matrix rows 24-31, register pair b6/b7):

Column 0:
  Rows 24-25: T0  holds {b6, b7}
  Rows 30-31: T3  holds {b6, b7}

Column 1:
  Rows 24-25: T4  holds {b6, b7}
  Rows 30-31: T7  holds {b6, b7}

Column 7:
  Rows 24-25: T28 holds {b6, b7}
  Rows 30-31: T31 holds {b6, b7}

=============================================================
THREAD GROUPING PATTERN
=============================================================
Threads are grouped in sets of 4 per column:
  Column 0: T0, T1, T2, T3  (rows 0-7 across all bands)
  Column 1: T4, T5, T6, T7
  Column 2: T8, T9, T10, T11
  Column 3: T12, T13, T14, T15
  Column 4: T16, T17, T18, T19
  Column 5: T20, T21, T22, T23
  Column 6: T24, T25, T26, T27
  Column 7: T28, T29, T30, T31

Within each column group of 4 threads:
  T(col*4+0) covers rows 0-1, 8-9, 16-17, 24-25
  T(col*4+1) covers rows 2-3, 10-11, 18-19, 26-27
  T(col*4+2) covers rows 4-5, 12-13, 20-21, 28-29
  T(col*4+3) covers rows 6-7, 14-15, 22-23, 30-31

=============================================================
COMPLETE REGISTER SUMMARY PER THREAD
=============================================================
Every thread T(n) holds 8 register values organized as 4 pairs:
  Register pair 1: {b0, b1}  — covers 2 rows in band rows 0-7
  Register pair 2: {b2, b3}  — covers 2 rows in band rows 8-15
  Register pair 3: {b4, b5}  — covers 2 rows in band rows 16-23
  Register pair 4: {b6, b7}  — covers 2 rows in band rows 24-31

Each pair is packed as two f16/bf16 values in one 32-bit register.
Total registers per thread for matrix B: 4 (each holding 2 f16/bf16 values = 8 elements total).


  Figure 121 Sparse MMA .m16n8k32 fragment layout for matrix B with `.f16`/`.bf16` type.
* Matrix fragments for accumulators C and D are the same as in case of
  [Matrix Fragments for mma.m16n8k16 with floating point type](#warp-level-matrix-fragment-mma-16816-float)
  for `.f16`/`.b16` formats.
* Metadata: A `.b32` register containing 16 2-bit vectors with each pair of 2-bit vectors storing
  the indices of two non-zero element from a 4-wide chunk of matrix A as shown in
  [Figure 122](#sparse-mma-metadata-16832-f16bf16).

  > 

[Figure: sparse-mma-metadata-16832-f16bf16]
SPARSE MMA METADATA LAYOUT: .m16n8k32 for .f16/.bf16 type

==============================================================
1. MMA SHAPE AND DATA TYPES
==============================================================
Shape: m16n8k32
Data types: .f16 (float16) and .bf16 (bfloat16)
Matrix A is 16 rows x 32 columns (sparse, 2:4 structured sparsity)
Metadata encodes which 2 of every 4 elements are non-zero

==============================================================
2. TABLE STRUCTURE
==============================================================
Rows: 0 through 15 (matrix rows, with rows 2-6 and 10-14 implied by pattern)
Columns: 0..3, 4..7, 8..11, 12..15, 16..19, 20..23, 24..27, 28..31
(These are K-dimension column groups, 32 total columns in groups of 4)

The table is divided into two horizontal bands:
- Upper band: Rows 0-7 (uses lower 16 bits of metadata register: bits [15..0])
- Lower band: Rows 8-15 (uses upper 16 bits of metadata register: bits [31..16])

==============================================================
3. COMPLETE THREAD AND BIT ASSIGNMENTS
==============================================================

UPPER BAND (Rows 0-7), using register bits [15..0]:

Row 0:
  Col 0..3:   T_{2i}   bits [3..0]
  Col 4..7:   T_{2i}   bits [7..4]
  Col 8..11:  T_{2i}   bits [11..8]
  Col 12..15: T_{2i}   bits [15..12]
  Col 16..19: T_{2i+1} bits [3..0]
  Col 20..23: T_{2i+1} bits [7..4]
  Col 24..27: T_{2i+1} bits [11..8]
  Col 28..31: T_{2i+1} bits [15..12]

Row 1:
  Col 0..3:   T_{2i+4}  bits [3..0]
  Col 4..7:   T_{2i+4}  bits [7..4]
  Col 8..11:  T_{2i+4}  bits [11..8]
  Col 12..15: T_{2i+4}  bits [15..12]
  Col 16..19: T_{2i+5}  bits [3..0]
  Col 20..23: T_{2i+5}  bits [7..4]
  Col 24..27: T_{2i+5}  bits [11..8]
  Col 28..31: T_{2i+5}  bits [15..12]

Rows 2-6: Follow the same pattern (implied by "..." notation), continuing the thread index progression in steps of +4 per row pair.

Row 7:
  Col 0..3:   T_{2i+28} bits [3..0]
  Col 4..7:   T_{2i+28} bits [7..4]
  Col 8..11:  T_{2i+28} bits [11..8]
  Col 12..15: T_{2i+28} bits [15..12]
  Col 16..19: T_{2i+29} bits [3..0]
  Col 20..23: T_{2i+29} bits [7..4]
  Col 24..27: T_{2i+29} bits [11..8]
  Col 28..31: T_{2i+29} bits [15..12]

LOWER BAND (Rows 8-15), using register bits [31..16]:

Row 8:
  Col 0..3:   T_{2i}   bits [19..16]
  Col 4..7:   T_{2i}   bits [23..20]
  Col 8..11:  T_{2i}   bits [27..24]
  Col 12..15: T_{2i}   bits [31..28]
  Col 16..19: T_{2i+1} bits [19..16]
  Col 20..23: T_{2i+1} bits [23..20]
  Col 24..27: T_{2i+1} bits [27..24]
  Col 28..31: T_{2i+1} bits [31..28]

Row 9:
  Col 0..3:   T_{2i+4}  bits [19..16]
  Col 4..7:   T_{2i+4}  bits [23..20]
  Col 8..11:  T_{2i+4}  bits [27..24]
  Col 12..15: T_{2i+4}  bits [31..28]
  Col 16..19: T_{2i+5}  bits [19..16]
  Col 20..23: T_{2i+5}  bits [23..20]
  Col 24..27: T_{2i+5}  bits [27..24]
  Col 28..31: T_{2i+5}  bits [31..28]

Rows 10-14: Follow the same pattern (implied by "..." notation).

Row 15:
  Col 0..3:   T_{2i+28} bits [19..16]
  Col 4..7:   T_{2i+28} bits [23..20]
  Col 8..11:  T_{2i+28} bits [27..24]
  Col 12..15: T_{2i+28} bits [31..28]
  Col 16..19: T_{2i+29} bits [19..16]
  Col 20..23: T_{2i+29} bits [23..20]
  Col 24..27: T_{2i+29} bits [27..24]
  Col 28..31: T_{2i+29} bits [31..28]

==============================================================
4. METADATA ENCODING FORMAT
==============================================================
Each group of 4 columns (e.g., cols 0..3) corresponds to 4 consecutive K-dimension elements.
For 2:4 sparsity, exactly 2 of every 4 elements are non-zero.
Each 4-bit field encodes which 2 elements (out of 4) are the non-zero ones.

Bit field width per column group: 4 bits
Each 4-bit field selects 2 non-zero positions out of 4 possible positions (indices 0,1,2,3).
The 4 bits are split into two 2-bit selectors:
  - Lower 2 bits [1..0]: index (0-3) of the first non-zero element in the group of 4
  - Upper 2 bits [3..2]: index (0-3) of the second non-zero element in the group of 4

Each thread holds a 32-bit metadata register:
  - Bits [15..0]:  metadata for rows 0-7 (upper band)
  - Bits [31..16]: metadata for rows 8-15 (lower band)

Within each 16-bit half:
  - Bits [3..0]:   column group 0..3
  - Bits [7..4]:   column group 4..7
  - Bits [11..8]:  column group 8..11
  - Bits [15..12]: column group 12..15

==============================================================
5. THREAD GROUPING AND INDEXING PATTERNS
==============================================================
The index variable i ranges such that thread pairs (T_{2i}, T_{2i+1}) cover columns 0..15 and 16..31 respectively for a given row.

Thread index progression across rows (upper band, rows 0-7):
  Row 0: threads T_{2i},   T_{2i+1}   (offset +0)
  Row 1: threads T_{2i+4}, T_{2i+5}   (offset +4)
  Row 2: threads T_{2i+8}, T_{2i+9}   (offset +8, implied)
  Row 3: threads T_{2i+12},T_{2i+13}  (offset +12, implied)
  Row 4: threads T_{2i+16},T_{2i+17}  (offset +16, implied)
  Row 5: threads T_{2i+20},T_{2i+21}  (offset +20, implied)
  Row 6: threads T_{2i+24},T_{2i+25}  (offset +24, implied)
  Row 7: threads T_{2i+28},T_{2i+29}  (offset +28)

The same thread index pattern repeats for the lower band (rows 8-15), but using the upper 16 bits [31..16] of the same threads' metadata registers.

Column grouping: 8 groups of 4 columns each (0..3, 4..7, 8..11, 12..15, 16..19, 20..23, 24..27, 28..31). The first 4 column groups (cols 0..15) are covered by even-indexed thread T_{2i+offset}, and the second 4 column groups (cols 16..31) are covered by odd-indexed thread T_{2i+offset+1}.

Each thread thus provides metadata for exactly one row and one half (left or right) of the 32 K-dimension columns, storing 4 bits per group-of-4 columns x 4 groups = 16 bits per row half, packed into a 32-bit register covering two rows (one from the upper band, one from the lower band).

  >
  >
  > Figure 122 Sparse MMA .m16n8k32 metadata layout for `.f16`/`.bf16` type.
